texto: But why was the room suddenly becoming so dark? It was the middle of the afternoon. With a supreme effort Giuseppe Corte, who felt himself paralyzed with a strange lethargy, looked at the clock on the nightstand beside the bed. It was 3:30. He turned his head in the other direction and saw that the shutters, in obedience to some mysterious command, were closing slowly, blocking the passage of light.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Dino Buzzati que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “But why was the room suddenly becoming so dark? It was the middle of the afternoon. With a suprem…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, death-and-dying, suddenly, com foco em reflexao-noturna e boa-noite.

frase_original: But why was the room suddenly becoming so dark? It was the middle of the afternoon. With a supreme effort Giuseppe Corte, who felt himself paralyzed with a strange lethargy, looked at the clock on the nightstand beside the bed. It was 3:30. He turned his head in the other direction and saw that the shutters, in obedience to some mysterious command, were closing slowly, blocking the passage of light.

texto: It was at this period that Drogo realised how far apart men are whatever their affection for each other, that if you suffer the pain is yours and yours alone, no one else can take upon himself the least part of it; that if you suffer it does not mean that others feel pain even though their love is great: hence the loneliness of life.
explicacao: Citação de Dino Buzzati sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “It was at this period that Drogo realised how far apart men are whatever their affection for each…” articula reflexao, buzzati, loneliness em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como loneliness e tartar-steppe.
